title: NOC 41201 – Post-Secondary Teaching and Research Assistants | GTR Immigration
description: Post-Secondary Teaching and Research Assistants under NOC 41201 support professors and academic departments in delivering high-quality education and conducting research across Canada’s colleges and universities. Classified as TEER 1 (Professional Occupation), this role is often filled by graduate students or academic professionals building careers in education and research. These positions are essential to Canada’s higher education system, assisting in teaching, laboratory supervision, academic grading, and scholarly research activities.

classification.teer: Classification LevelDetailsBroad Occupation Category4 – Occupations in education, law, and social, community, and government servicesTEER1 – Occupations usually require a university degreeMajor Group41 – Professional occupations in law, education, social, community and government servicesSub-Major Group412 – Professional occupations in education servicesMinor Group4120 – University professors and post-secondary assistantsNOC Code41201

duties: Organize reference materials and visual aids
Conduct seminars, discussion groups, and laboratory sessions
Assist in examination preparation and administration
Grade examinations, term papers, and laboratory reports
Perform laboratory experiments and data collection
Support preparation of journal articles and research papers
employment_requirements: Enrollment in, or completion of, a university or college program
Graduate-level study (often required for research assistant roles)
Subject specialization relevant to academic department
International candidates may need credential assessment for immigration purposes.
salary_prospects: Post-Secondary Teaching and Research Assistants typically earn between:
$25,100 – $86,800 per year, depending on experience, institution, and specialization.
job_outlook: The outlook for NOC 41201 is stable and slightly positive due to:
Expansion of STEM and applied research programs
These roles are often contract-based but provide valuable Canadian academic experience.
labour_market_demand: Post-Secondary Teaching and Research Assistants are employed in:
Opportunities are available nationwide, particularly in Ontario, Quebec, British Columbia, and Alberta.
Opportunity to gain Canadian teaching experience
Potential pathway to permanent academic roles
Many individuals use this role as a stepping stone toward becoming University Professors (NOC 41200).
pathways: Post-Secondary Teaching and Research Assistants qualify under high-skilled TEER 1 categories and may apply through:
Foreign students already studying in Canada often use this occupation to strengthen their immigration profile.
